MAY BREAK THE FEDERATION.


The action of the delegates of the
Butte miners union in withdrawing
from the convention of the West
ern Federation Of Miners at Den
yer comes as surprise to local
members of organized labor. The
Butte union was the first of the
western unions to urge the forma-
ton of the Western Federation and
1t has been the foundation stone
of the big organization. The Butte
miners are regarded as being con.
sentative, and have had no trou.
BILE of any kind for the last 20
years. When the big fight was on
between Heinze and the Amalga.
mated Copper company some years
ago the Butte union took $50, 900
in Amalgamated stock to show the
outside world their confidence in
the mines.


Just what effect the action of the
Butte union will have on the fu-
ture Of the Western Federation,
should It adhere to its present reso-
